patterns_recognized: All five picks today arrive as tier=SKIP because config.STAKING_ENABLED=False (model under repair, staked-tier AUC 0.48 < coin flip) — this is a system-level gate, not a per-game signal failure; claude_tier below reflects directional/quality conviction independent of the staking freeze.

Negative-edge picks outside the [4,15]pp staking band (SD@ATL -12.62pp, KC@DET -14.31pp) mirror the Chris Sale/ATL negative-edge pattern validated twice in the archive (6/4, 7/10 CAP1-caught losses; Sale/ATL only won on 7/17 when the edge was positive) — a reliable non-bettable signal regardless of who wins on the field.

TB@TOR: the 7/21 postgame log explicitly flagged a DOWNGRADE that day ('market has priced in the edge') as an unvalidated, factually-wrong heuristic that missed a 12-2 blowout win, in direct violation of the 7/20 mandate to lean CONFIRM on GOLD-shape picks absent a >=70%-validated suppression rule — applying that lesson today.

ARI@STL's F3_swing_take_gap (5165.2) is the strongest CAP10-rescue anchor seen in the recent archive, clearing the >1000 threshold that separated 7/21's one winning rescue (SD@ATL, F3=2777.6) from its two losing rescues (F3=822.2 and 1761.1) by a wide margin.

MIN@CLE is a same-matchup rematch of 7/21 (CLE won 5-2 with an identical bottom-bucket SKIP/CONFIRM disposition explicitly validated in that postgame file); today's SP-xERA signal additionally opposes the CLE pick, reinforcing the same read.

SD @ ATL.reasoning: Edge is -12.62pp, well outside the staking band (market implies 73.6% for ATL vs our model's 60.9%) — this magnitude of model/market disagreement echoes the Sale/ATL negative-edge pattern that correctly flagged CAP1-style losses on 6/4 and 7/10 (Sale/ATL only won 7/17 with a positive edge). SD just routed ATL 8-3 in this same series two days ago (7/21). Bullpen gap (-0.02) and lineup concentration are roughly neutral. SKIP is the right call regardless of on-field outcome.

MIN @ CLE.reasoning: SP-xERA actually favors Taj Bradley over Gavin Williams (-2 score, opposes the CLE pick), and pick_prob (0.519-0.536 depending on blend) sits in the bottom bucket that HARD CAP 10 zeroes absent a CONFIRM. This is the identical MIN@CLE matchup from 7/21, where CLE won 5-2 and the same SKIP disposition was explicitly validated in that postgame file. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (+0.025) also mildly favors MIN's bullpen. Thin edge, opposing SP signal, and a directly-validated precedent all point the same way.

TB @ TOR.reasoning: Edge is thin (+0.66pp) and HARD CAP 10 zeroes the stake, but the directional read is sound. TB has beaten TOR in two of the last three meetings including a 12-2 blowout on 7/21 — the postgame log explicitly flagged that day's DOWNGRADE ('market has priced in the edge') as an unvalidated, factually-wrong heuristic that missed the win. PQI confirms TB (Δ=-6.2) and the F-signal fires. No stake either way given the config-wide staking freeze, but I lean with TB rather than fight the pick.

ARI @ STL.reasoning: Pre-cap this graded B+ (score 3) before HARD CAP 10 zeroed it for lacking a CONFIRM. F3_swing_take_gap is 5165.2 — well above the >1000 anchor that separated 7/21's one winning CAP10 rescue (SD@ATL, F3=2777.6) from its two losses (F3=822/1761), and the strongest such reading in recent memory. SP-xERA (1.13, Pfaadt over McGreevy) and PQI (Δ=-19.7) both agree with ARI, and the PLATINUM→GOLD guardrail demotion already addressed overconfidence risk. Marking claude_tier GOLD to flag this as the slate's best directional read, though nothing stakes given the global config freeze.

KC @ DET.reasoning: DET is picked (56.9% model prob) but the market prices them far richer (71.2% implied), a -14.31pp gap outside the staking band — comparable in size to the Sale/ATL negative-edge pattern that has proven a reliable non-bettable signal. DET is playing well (11-2 and 8-6 wins in its last two logged games) so a DET win on the field wouldn't be surprising, but hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap (+0.012) and a Stage 1/2 disagreement (Δ=0.13, not offset by a >20pp PQI edge) add caution. The price here isn't offering value regardless of outcome; CONFIRM SKIP.
